arts.ttl: Sam Allardyce praises Arsenal's use of 'dark arts' against Man City and recalls the time Liverpool reported him for using similar tactics with Bolton
arts.sum: Allardyce clearly approved of how Arteta's men stifled City's flow, having deployed similar tactics against some of the Premier League's biggest teams while in charge of Bolton in the 2000s.

arts.ttl: Manchester City's plan to cope with Rodri injury as Pep Guardiola weighs up options
arts.sum: Man City are facing up to the devastating news that midfielder Rodri could miss the rest of the season, after suffering a suspected torn ACL ligament. How will Pep Guardiola and the reigning Premier League champions cope without such an influential presence?

arts.ttl: When Watford stunned the European champions in the League Cup
arts.sum: A look back to when Watford shocked then European champions Nottingham Forest in the League Cup in 1980.

arts.ttl: Man City transfer news: Pep Guardiola wants Inter Milan star 'at any cost'
arts.sum: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola is reportedly interested in signing an Inter Milan player 'at any cost' during the next summer transfer window.

arts.ttl: Arteta responds to 'dark arts' taunts by Man City as injuries mount for Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta bristled at suggestions his team used “dark arts” in the feisty English Premier League match at Manchester City, saying he preferred to look at “facts” rather than opinions. This development came after Arsenal players were accused of play-acting and feigning injuries by some of their Man City counterparts during the 2-2 draw on Sunday at Etihad Stadium.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta suggests Arsenal player suffered 'serious injury' in Man City draw
arts.sum: Arsenal's understandably defensive performance was criticised by City players, who bemoaned of the visitors' 'dark arts' and time-wasting tactics used to try and run the clock down. Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has claimed at least one of his players came away from Sunday's 2-2 draw at Manchester City with a'serious injury'.

arts.ttl: Why Man City are playing in Carabao Cup tonight just 48 hours after Arsenal clash
arts.sum: Latest Manchester City news as Pep Guardiola and Co. will face Watford in the Carabao Cup just two days after the draw with Arsenal in the Premier League

arts.ttl: Arsenal revealed as Premier League's biggest time-wasters after Man City clash
arts.sum: Arsenal's tactics against Manchester City in Sunday's dramatic draw have come under intense scrutiny, with a series of Pep Guardiola's players hitting out at the Gunners

arts.ttl: Supercomputer predicts how many points Rodri injury will cost Man City in title race
arts.sum: Early leaders Manchester City are expected to be without influential midfielder Rodri for the rest of the season, handing rivals Arsenal a major advantage in the title race

arts.ttl: How Manchester City’s ‘little Lahm’ can help fill the void left by Rodri | Ben McAleer
arts.sum: Champions will miss their midfield linchpin but Rico Lewis is a versatile ball-winner who could step in and shine By Ben McAleer for WhoScored? It was the news Manchester City feared – Rodri may miss the rest of the season. Only 21 minutes into his first Premier League start of the campaign, the Spaniard went down clutching his right knee. The sight of their midfield linchpin on the ground in agony is what supporters feared most – and the signs are that the 28-year-old has suffered anterior cruciate ligament damage. While City haven’t exactly been underwhelming in the absence of Rodri – they have made an unbeaten start to the season after all – over the course of a season, Pep Guardiola will sorely miss the brain of his side. The return of Ilkay Gündogan eases the absence of Rodri but it’s the emergence of another player that will be a boost to City. Continue reading...
